notes: Issue #41, authored by Claude (Copilot budget exhausted). LIVE recipe recipes/ind_presidentofindia.yml over presidentofindia.nic.in/speeches. Probe --spread: 20 links, title(h1.title.page-title)/text(.views-field-nothing)/date all green, parsed 2026-06-02..2026-07-15. WHY partial (NOT a fixable selector issue): the Drupal view is RECENT-ONLY — ?page=0 and ?page=1 give 10 links each, ?page=2 gives 0, i.e. ~20 items = the trailing ~6 weeks. The exposed filter form has field_speeches_date_value but it is an <input type=date> EXACT-date filter and every older date tried (2026-05-09, 2026-03-10, 2025-08-15, 2024-01-26, 2023-06-15, 2022-07-25) returns 0; the sibling host www.presidentofindia.gov.in serves the same view with the same 2-page limit; no sitemap (/sitemap.xml 404). Good as a live/incremental source, not a history — same shape as bgd_bss_president. UA-KEYED WAF: honest bot UA gets HTTP 403 (377-byte body), browser UA gets 200 -> per-recipe user_agent override set. DATE CORRECTNESS: the only date is a numeric DD.MM.YYYY dateline in the body; dateparser resolves ambiguous numerics by locale, so date_languages MUST be [hi] — with [en] (or [hi,en]) '01.07.2026' parses as 2026-01-07 (month-first, WRONG); with [hi] it parses as 2026-07-01. Verified against dateparser directly. Every date with day<=12 would silently corrupt otherwise. MIXED LANGUAGE: the view interleaves English and Hindi renderings of the same address as separate nodes, so a full run yields near-duplicate pairs; downstream de-dup concern. FOLLOW-UP: an ind_presidentofindia_wayback companion is needed for the history (IA holds the site) and can reuse these selectors. country=India; source_url=https://presidentofindia.nic.in/speeches; source_name=presidentofindia.nic.in; source_type=official_gov; region=Asia; iso3n=356; content_format=fulltext. FULL RUN pending (researcher gate).
